message:
chore: close bead ft-3681t.4.5 (robot API contract matrix)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2793,7 +2793,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-3681t.4.2.1","title":"[ft-3681t.4.2][support] Preserve robot-events cursor checkpoint across empty replay pages","description":"Cursor-mode `ft robot events` currently returns `next_cursor = null` when a replay page is empty, which weakens checkpoint-resume semantics compared with the audit stream path. Update the response contract so empty cursor pages preserve the caller checkpoint, and add focused regressions for empty and non-empty cursor pages plus argument/error behavior.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlBarn","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:07:50.014034Z","created_by":"PearlBarn","updated_at":"2026-03-10T05:42:34.358065Z","closed_at":"2026-03-10T05:32:58.976714Z","close_reason":"Restored workspace stack override and revalidated robot_events cursor/argument test slice via rch exec; support bead complete","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contracts","events","robot-api","streaming"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.2.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:07:50.014034Z","created_by":"PearlBarn","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1926,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.2.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented the robot-events cursor checkpoint fix in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs and added focused helper regressions. Verification is currently blocked by unrelated dirty-worktree compile failures in crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs, crates/frankenterm-core/src/fleet_launcher.rs, and crates/frankenterm-core/src/swarm_pipeline.rs during rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pearlbarn-target cargo test -p frankenterm robot_events_ -- --nocapture.","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:23:52Z"},{"id":1930,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.2.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Patch-specific tests are green in the warmed isolated target: robot_events_next_cursor_preserves_checkpoint_for_empty_replay_page, robot_events_next_cursor_advances_to_last_event_id, robot_events_cursor_validation_rejects_negative_values, and robot_events_replay_limit_validation_rejects_zero all passed. A broader robot_events_ filter hit an unrelated pre-existing stack overflow in tests::cli_robot_events_dry_run_flag. Workspace cargo check advanced deep into the graph but failed on environment capacity: /tmp/ft-pearlbarn-target ran out of disk space while compiling frankenterm-core.","created_at":"2026-03-07T06:43:49Z"},{"id":2071,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.2.1","author":"FoggyRaven","text":"FoggyRaven follow-up (2026-03-10): diagnosed the remaining failure as missing workspace stack override rather than cursor-contract logic. Restored .cargo/config.toml with RUST_MIN_STACK=16777216 (matching the previously-disabled repo config) and revalidated via rch exec using isolated target /tmp/ft-foggyraven-robot-events. Evidence: cargo test -p frankenterm robot_events_next_cursor_ -- --nocapture ✅; cargo test -p frankenterm robot_events_cursor_validation_rejects_negative_values -- --nocapture ✅; cargo test -p frankenterm robot_events_replay_limit_validation_rejects_zero -- --nocapture ✅; cargo test -p frankenterm cli_robot_events_cursor_and_replay_limit_parse -- --nocapture ✅; cargo test -p frankenterm robot_events_ -- --nocapture ✅ (9 unit tests + contract_robot_events_mutations_roundtrip). Remote rch workers still failed SSH and fell back locally, but all invocations were made through rch exec per repo policy.","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:32:37Z"},{"id":2075,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.2.1","author":"FoggyRaven","text":"Follow-up evidence: removed the .gitignore rule that kept .cargo/config.toml ignored, so the restored 16MB RUST_MIN_STACK override is now trackable. Revalidated with rch: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-foggyraven-robot-events cargo test -p frankenterm cli_robot_events_cursor_and_replay_limit_parse -- --nocapture => passed.","created_at":"2026-03-10T05:42:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.4.3","title":"[robot] Transactional action engine (dry-run, approval, rollback)","description":"Build transaction-like action semantics for high-impact operations: dry-run previews, policy checks, approval checkpoints, execution receipts, and rollback strategies when possible.","acceptance_criteria":"1. High-impact commands support dry-run and approval gates. 2. Execution receipts include enough detail for audit and debugging. 3. Rollback/compensation behavior is explicit.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkSpire","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:12:48.134973Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:45:00.000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["robot-api","safety","transactions"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:12:48.134973Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:16:58.893063Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.3","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:16:59.365103Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1998,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.3","author":"PinkSpire","text":"Implemented tx-engine semantics in crates/frankenterm-core/src/plan.rs plus CLI/MCP parity wiring: commit now validates lifecycle state, emits deterministic per-step receipts, records failure_boundary, blocks via explicit KillSwitchBlocked/PauseSuspended outcomes instead of hard errors, and only auto-compensates when there are committed steps to unwind. Compensation now validates lifecycle state, runs in reverse commit order, emits receipts, and reports compensated/failed/skipped counts. Updated tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_interfaces.sh assertions for explicit commit/rollback counts/receipts and added paused + safe-mode scenarios. Verification via rch with isolated caches: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib tx_ -- --nocapture passed (145 passed, includes new plan.rs tests); cargo test -p frankenterm mission_robot_tx_helpers_build_expected_inputs_and_reports -- --nocapture passed. Deterministic e2e script currently fails before exercising tx logic because remote rch cargo run cannot resolve sibling path dependency /home/ubuntu/rch/frankenterm/frankenredis/crates/fr-config on the worker; artifacts in tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_interfaces_20260307_185929.*.","created_at":"2026-03-08T00:05:35Z"}],"close_reason":"Implemented by PinkSpire in plan.rs: dry-run (DispatchDryRun), approval gates (KillSwitchBlocked/PauseSuspended), execution receipts (TxCommitReport with per-step receipts), rollback strategies. 4 tests verified passing."}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.4.4","title":"[robot] Machine contracts, SDK generation, ntm-compat shim, replay tests","description":"Publish durable machine contracts (schemas/specs/examples), generate client SDK surfaces, add an ntm compatibility shim for migration acceleration, and enforce behavior via replay-based contract tests.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Contracts and generated clients stay in lockstep with implementation. 2. Compatibility shim supports migration-critical workflows. 3. Replay tests catch protocol regressions.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:12:54.039676Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T19:31:27.907853Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contracts","migration","robot-api"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:12:54.039676Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:16:59.839104Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:00.324144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.4","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:00.801484Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-3681t.4.6","title":"[robot] Idempotency keys, dedupe, and at-least-once safe mutation semantics","description":"Add idempotency and deduplication semantics for mutating robot actions to make retries safe under network failures, reconnects, and orchestrator restarts.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Mutating robot APIs support idempotency keys and deterministic replay-safe outcomes. 2. Duplicate request handling is explicit and observable. 3. Failure/retry semantics prevent unintended repeated side effects. Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic. Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario. Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case. Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code. Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:34:39.460278Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-11T18:40:00.000000Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["idempotency","reliability","robot-api"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.3.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:35:15.275014Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:34:39.460278Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:35:13.566568Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:35:14.663186Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.6","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:35:15.951847Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"assignee":"PinkForge","resolution":"Already implemented: tx_idempotency module provides IdempotencyKey, DeduplicationGuard, TxExecutionLedger, ResumeContext, hash-chain verification, and IdempotencyPolicy. Robot-serializable types in robot_types.rs.","close_reason":"Implemented in 20c443a0: MutationKey, MutationGuard, MutationOutcome with TTL eviction, FIFO capacity, batch checks, 35+ tests"}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.5","title":"[epic] Flywheel connector fabric as native FrankenTerm subsystem","description":"# Epic 5: Flywheel Connector Fabric Integration

Integrate flywheel connector system components as a first-class FrankenTerm subsystem: host runtime, registry, sandboxing, lifecycle, event bridges, mesh federation, and audit chain.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Connectors are installable/runnable under policy and capability constraints.
2. Bi-directional event flow between FrankenTerm and connectors is reliable.
3. Multi-host connector federation is operationally viable.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:44.148717Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T19:30:46.568150Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","epic","fcp-integration"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:44.148717Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:49.741297Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:50.159223Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1722,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Design note: FCP integration is deep-by-design. We are not adding one-off connectors; we are bringing host/runtime/security/mesh/audit primitives into FrankenTerm so external system automation is governed by the same policy and telemetry contracts as terminal actions.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:56Z"}],"assignee":"PinkForge"}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.5.1","title":"[connectors] Embed FCP host/protocol runtime into FrankenTerm","description":"Integrate core flywheel connector runtime components (fcp-core, fcp-protocol, fcp-host) as native FrankenTerm services with explicit host lifecycle control, resource-isolation budgets, and deterministic failure containment rather than external bolt-ons.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Connector host runtime is runnable and observable inside FrankenTerm with explicit health/liveness signals.
2. Protocol boundaries between control plane and connectors are explicit, versioned, and testable.
3. Failure isolation and resource budgeting (CPU/memory/IO limits) are enforced to protect terminal responsiveness.
4. Host lifecycle operations (start/stop/restart/upgrade) are deterministic and safe under degraded conditions.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LilacCreek","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:13:00.788412Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-03T22:28:56.360503Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T22:28:56.360435Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp-integration","host-runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:01.357088Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:01.859615Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:13:00.788412Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1768,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Implemented connector host runtime slice (lifecycle/protocol envelope/budget guardrails) with unit tests and deterministic e2e harness; e2e pass artifact at tests/e2e/logs/ft_3681t_5_1_20260303_000544.jsonl. Validation attempts run via rch exec: cargo check/clippy/fmt currently fail on pre-existing workspace issues outside this bead (frankenterm-gui unresolved k9/benchmarking, frankenterm-core bench WaModel/WaMsg gating, blob-leases clippy errors, broad fmt drift). Keeping bead in_progress pending policy on global blockers vs slice-local close.","created_at":"2026-03-03T05:32:44Z"},{"id":1775,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Added integration test coverage for connector host runtime degraded/recovery contracts in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/connector_host_runtime_integration.rs and extended tests/e2e/test_ft_3681t_5_1_connector_host_runtime.sh with integration-phase logging + FT_SKIP_LIB_TESTS/FT_CARGO_TARGET_DIR controls for deterministic artifacting under multi-agent contention. Validation: rch exec with isolated /tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR passed integration tests once (2 passed). Subsequent reruns are currently blocked by unrelated in-flight compile break in crates/frankenterm-core/src/fleet_launcher.rs (missing LaunchOutcome fields bootstrap_dispatches/pre_launch_checkpoint) from concurrent work.","created_at":"2026-03-03T06:06:34Z"},{"id":1823,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.5.1","author":"SapphireHarbor","text":"Verification: connector_host_runtime 9/9 tests pass. cargo check --lib passes clean. Integration tests in tests/connector_host_runtime_integration.rs also compile and pass.","created_at":"2026-03-03T22:28:52Z"}]}
